Record Class Union<A,B>

java.lang.Object
java.lang.Record
de.uka.ilkd.key.util.Union<A,B>
Type Parameters:
A - the type for the first alternative
B - the type for the second alternative
public record Union<A,B>(Object value, boolean isFirst) extends Record
A union type contains a value that can be one of two types. It is also called the "sum" type sometimes. Union containers are immutable.

A union element contains a single reference that can be of type A or of type B. The pure functions isFirst() and isSecond() can be used to check if stored reference is of the respective types. The functions getFirst() and getSecond() can be used to retrieve the value with the respective type. An exception is raised if getFirst() is invoked while isFirst() returns false.

See Also:
  • Optional
  • Pair

  • Constructor Details

    • Union

      public Union(Object value, boolean isFirst)
      private constructor the static methods fromFirst(Object) and fromSecond(Object).
      Parameters:
      value - the value to store, may be null
      isFirst - true if of first, false if of second type

  • Method Details

    • fromFirst

      public static <A,B> Union<A,B> fromFirst(A value)
      Instantiate a new union type with the value stored from the first type.

      The result will return true for isFirst().

      Type Parameters:
      A - the type of the first alternative
      B - the type of the second alternative
      Parameters:
      value - the value to store, may be null
      Returns:
      a freshly created immutable union object.

    • fromSecond

      public static <A,B> Union<A,B> fromSecond(B value)
      Instantiate a new union type with the value stored from the second type.

      The result will return true for isSecond().

      Type Parameters:
      A - the type of the first alternative
      B - the type of the second alternative
      Parameters:
      value - the value to store, may be null
      Returns:
      a freshly created immutable union object.

    • isFirst

      public boolean isFirst()
      Returns if this union object has been created using fromFirst(Object).
      Returns:
      true iff this union object has created using fromFirst(Object).

    • isSecond

      public boolean isSecond()
      Returns if this union object has been created using fromSecond(Object).
      Returns:
      true iff this union object has created using fromSecond(Object).

    • getFirst

      public A getFirst()
      Returns the stored value (of type A) if this object has been created using fromFirst(Object). Throws a NoSuchElementException otherwise.
      Returns:
      the stored value, may be null

    • getSecond

      public B getSecond()
      Returns the stored value (of type B) if this object has been created using fromSecond(Object). Throws a NoSuchElementException otherwise.
      Returns:
      the stored value, may be null

    • mapFirst

      public <C> Union<C,B> mapFirst(Function<A,C> function)
      Create a new union object. If isFirst() returns true, then the function is applied to the value, if not the value remains untouched.
      Type Parameters:
      C - the result type of function
      Parameters:
      function - non-null function to apply to the value
      Returns:
      a fresh union object

    • mapSecond

      public <C> Union<A,C> mapSecond(Function<B,C> function)
      Create a new union object. If isSecond() returns true, then the function is applied to the value, if not the value remains untouched.
      Type Parameters:
      C - the result type of function
      Parameters:
      function - non-null function to apply to the value
      Returns:
      a fresh union object

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Returns a string representation of this record class. The representation contains the name of the class, followed by the name and value of each of the record components.
      Specified by:
      toString in class Record
      Returns:
      a string representation of this object

    • hashCode

      public final int hashCode()
      Returns a hash code value for this object. The value is derived from the hash code of each of the record components.
      Specified by:
      hashCode in class Record
      Returns:
      a hash code value for this object

    • equals

      public final boolean equals(Object o)
      Indicates whether some other object is "equal to" this one. The objects are equal if the other object is of the same class and if all the record components are equal. Reference components are compared with Objects::equals(Object,Object); primitive components are compared with the compare method from their corresponding wrapper classes.
      Specified by:
      equals in class Record
      Parameters:
      o - the object with which to compare
      Returns:
      true if this object is the same as the o argument; false otherwise.

    • value

      public Object value()
      Returns the value of the value record component.
      Returns:
      the value of the value record component
